title: milium vulgare var. album
intro: Milium vulgare var. album, also known as white millet, is a type of flowering plant in the grass family, characterized by its small white or yellowish flowers and slender stems. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. The health benefits of Milium vulgare var. album include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, regulate blood sugar, and provide antioxidant properties. The herbal preparations of this plant can be consumed as tea, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
